seo.title: 20 Best Things To Do in Rayong (2026 Guide) — Koh Samet, Beaches & Fruit Orchards
seo.metaDescription: Discover the 20 best things to do in Rayong in 2026. From Koh Samet's white beaches to Mae Ramphueng, tropical fruit orchards, and Sunthorn Phu's birthplace. Real prices and insider tips.

intro: Rayong flies under the radar of most foreign Thailand itineraries, and that's precisely its appeal. Bangkok weekenders have known for decades what overseas visitors overlook: this Gulf coast province has one of Thailand's most beautiful national park islands (Koh Samet), the longest uncrowded beach on the eastern seaboard (Mae Ramphueng), the country's best tropical fruit season (May-July), and a seafood scene that has no need to cater to tourists. Here are 20 things that make Rayong worth the three-hour drive from Bangkok.

activities.name: Take a Ferry to Koh Samet
activities.description: Rayong's star attraction — a national park island with brilliant white sand and calm, clear water just 45 minutes from Ban Phe pier. Hat Sai Kaew (White Sand Beach) on the north end is the busiest and most developed; Ao Phai and Ao Tubtim are mid-island with good snorkeling; Ao Cho and Ao Nuan in the south are quieter and more secluded. All beaches are within the Khao Laem Ya-Mu Ko Samet National Park.
activities.price: 50-70 THB slow ferry one-way + 200 THB national park fee (~$7-8 USD total)
tips: Slow ferry from Nuanthip Pier: 50-70 THB (40 min). Fast catamaran: 150 THB (15 min). Speedboat: 150-300 THB (10 min)
National park fee is 200 THB for foreigners — valid for one week, covers Koh Samet and Khao Laem Ya mainland
Stay overnight to enjoy the island after day-trippers leave — evenings at Hat Sai Kaew are magical with fire shows
activities.bestTime: November to April (dry season best, but accessible year-round)

activities.name: Walk Mae Ramphueng Beach
activities.description: Thailand's longest Gulf coast beach — a 12 km strip of soft khaki sand stretching from Ban Phe to Laem Mae Phim. Almost entirely overlooked by foreign tourists, this is where Bangkok families unwind. The water is calm and shallow, ideal for swimming. Beachside restaurants serve freshly grilled seafood at local prices (100-300 THB per dish). The scale is extraordinary — stretches of near-empty beach even on weekends.
tips: The stretch near the Mae Ramphueng OTOP market has the best seafood restaurants
Rent a bicycle from a beach vendor (50-100 THB/hour) to cover more of the 12 km
Morning is best for swimming — afternoons bring sea breezes

activities.name: Visit Koh Munnork (Island Day Trip)
activities.description: A small, pristine island off the coast of Laem Mae Phim with crystal-clear water and good coral reefs for snorkeling. Far quieter than Koh Samet and doesn't require paying the national park fee. Speedboats depart from Mae Phim pier. The island has basic facilities — bring snacks and enough water. Excellent for snorkeling and photography.
tips: Charter a speedboat from Mae Phim pier — negotiate for 500-800 THB return for a group
Bring your own snorkeling gear — rental is limited on the island
The island has no national park fee unlike Koh Samet

activities.name: Explore the Tropical Fruit Orchards
activities.description: Rayong is one of Thailand's premier tropical fruit regions. Between May and July, the roadsides around Wang Chan, Klaeng, and Ban Khai are lined with stalls and roadside markets selling durian, rambutan, mangosteen, longan, and lychee at orchard-direct prices. The prized Mon Thong (golden pillow) durian variety from Rayong commands premium prices in Bangkok markets but sells for a fraction at the source.
tips: Durian season peaks in May-June; rambutan and mangosteen peak in June-July
Mon Thong (golden pillow) and Chanee are the two best Rayong durian varieties
Ask roadside sellers to open a durian before you buy — the smell inside should be rich and creamy, not sour

activities.name: Visit Sunthorn Phu Park
activities.description: A whimsical coastal park honoring Thailand's most celebrated poet, Sunthorn Phu (1786-1855), who was born in Ban Kram village on the Rayong coast. Giant statues of characters from his epic poem Phra Aphai Mani — a mermaid, a giant, a hermit, and the prince himself — dot the park's grounds with sea views behind them. A pilgrimage for Thai literature enthusiasts and a genuinely charming public park for everyone else.
tips: The mermaid statue facing the sea is the signature photo spot
Visit early morning when the light is best and the park is quiet
Combine with Mae Ramphueng Beach — the park is about 10 km east of Ban Phe

activities.name: Snorkel at Koh Samet's Ao Phai Beach
activities.description: Ao Phai Beach on Koh Samet's mid-east coast offers the best snorkeling on the island, with healthy coral visible from the beach. The bay is sheltered, water is clear, and the beach is less busy than Hat Sai Kaew (White Sand Beach). Snorkel gear rental costs 100-200 THB/hour from beach vendors. A 10-minute walk south of Ao Phai leads to the quieter Ao Tubtim.
activities.price: 100-200 THB gear rental (plus ferry and park fee)
tips: Walk south from Ao Phai to Ao Tubtim for a quieter beach with equally good snorkeling
The coral is best in 2-4 meters of water at the southern end of Ao Phai
Best snorkeling visibility is in the morning before the afternoon wind picks up

activities.name: Shop at Ban Phe Market
activities.description: The Ban Phe market area near the ferry piers is a must-stop for Rayong specialties. Stalls sell dried squid (pla muek haeng) — the most famous Rayong product — along with shrimp paste (kapi), nam pla fish sauce, dried seafood, and fresh tropical fruit. Bangkok day-trippers returning from Koh Samet always stock up here. The morning market (5-9 AM) is the most atmospheric with fresh seafood from overnight fishing boats.
activities.price: Free to browse (dried squid 100-400 THB per pack)
tips: Rayong dried squid is famous across Thailand — buy several vacuum-sealed packs for gifts
Compare prices between stalls — quality is similar, prices vary by 20-30%
Fresh seafood in the morning market is the cheapest you'll find on the eastern seaboard
activities.bestTime: Morning (5-9 AM) for freshest seafood; any time for dried goods

activities.name: Hike in Khao Laem Ya National Park
activities.description: The mainland portion of the Khao Laem Ya-Mu Ko Samet National Park — a forested headland near Ban Phe with walking trails through lowland jungle to a lighthouse with panoramic sea views. The park is rarely crowded compared to Koh Samet. Good birdwatching with kingfishers, hornbills, and various seabirds. The same 200 THB entrance fee covers both the mainland park and Koh Samet.
tips: The main trail to the headland lighthouse is about 2.5 km — moderate difficulty with some steep sections
Open 6 AM-5 PM; arrive early for birdwatching
The same entrance ticket covers Koh Samet if you visit the same week — keep your ticket

activities.name: Visit Rayong Aquarium
activities.description: A government-run aquarium in Rayong City operated by the Department of Fisheries, with displays of Thailand's Gulf coast marine life. Highlights include a glass walk-through tunnel with fish swimming overhead and touch pools with sea creatures. More educational than touristy — exhibits explain local fisheries and marine ecosystems. Popular with Thai families.
tips: Opening hours: Tuesday-Sunday 8:30 AM-4:30 PM (closed Monday)
Located on Sukhumvit Road in Rayong City — combine with a visit to the city
The fish feeding times (ask staff) are the most entertaining for children

activities.name: Water Sports at Laem Mae Phim Beach
activities.description: Laem Mae Phim is a curving bay beach south of Mae Ramphueng with a range of water sports available from vendors on the sand. Jet skiing, banana boat rides, kayaking, and windsurfing are all offered. The bay's natural shelter makes it calmer than open beaches and better suited for watersport activities. The Rayong Marriott Resort & Spa is located here, and their beach facilities raise the overall standard.
activities.price: Kayak 100-200 THB/hr, jet ski 800-1,200 THB/30 min, banana boat 200-300 THB/ride
tips: Morning is calmest for water sports — afternoons can be windy
Jet ski operators at this beach are among the more honest on the eastern seaboard — prices are posted
The Rayong Marriott beach bar is open to non-guests — a good spot for a drink after water sports

activities.name: Watch the Sunrise at Hat Sai Kaew, Koh Samet
activities.description: Hat Sai Kaew (White Sand Beach) on Koh Samet faces east, making it the best beach on the island for sunrise. The white quartz sand (the finest on any Thai island) reflects the early morning light beautifully. Stay overnight and wake up early — the beach is serene and almost empty before the day-trippers arrive on the 7-8 AM ferries.
tips: Sunrise is around 6:00-6:30 AM throughout the year
The beach restaurants start setting up by 6:30 AM — breakfast on the sand is a treat
Hat Sai Kaew's sand is notably whiter and finer than most Thai beaches — it's real quartz sand

activities.name: Eat Fresh Seafood at Ban Phe Pier Restaurants
activities.description: The string of open-air seafood restaurants along the Ban Phe pier road serves some of the freshest and cheapest grilled seafood on Thailand's eastern seaboard. Choose your fish, squid, or prawns from the ice display and have them grilled over charcoal while you wait. The setting — plastic tables facing the pier, boats coming and going — is unfussy but the food is outstanding. Tom yum goong (spicy prawn soup) here uses prawns straight from the overnight catch.
activities.price: 150-400 THB per person for a full seafood meal (~$4-11 USD)
tips: Arrive before the Koh Samet ferry crowds (before 8 AM) for the freshest morning catch
Hor mok talay (seafood curry steamed in banana leaf) and hoi malaeng puu (mussels with lemongrass) are local specialties
Prices increase 20-30% on weekends when Bangkok visitors pack the pier

activities.name: Day Trip to Koh Munnork Marine Park
activities.description: Koh Mun Nork is the largest of three small islands off the coast near Laem Mae Phim, known for its turtle conservation project and excellent snorkeling. Unlike Koh Samet (which requires a national park fee), Koh Munnork is managed differently and is less visited. The coral reefs are in better condition than much of Koh Samet. Green sea turtles have been spotted by snorkelers on the eastern side.
tips: Organize speedboats from Laem Mae Phim pier or through Rayong Marriott Resort activities desk
The turtle nesting beach on the north side is accessible but stay back from nesting areas
Bring reef-safe sunscreen only — chemical sunscreens are banned in all Thai national park waters

activities.name: Tour a Nam Pla (Fish Sauce) Factory
activities.description: Rayong is the capital of Thailand's nam pla (fish sauce) production — over 50 factories in the province ferment fish with salt in giant clay urns for 12-24 months to create the backbone flavoring of Thai cuisine. Tiparos and Megachef, two well-known Thai brands, have facilities in the area. Some producers welcome visitors for informal tours of the fermentation vats.
tips: The smell around active fermentation facilities is powerful — approach this with curiosity, not a sensitive nose
Nam pla made in Rayong is considered the finest in Thailand — buy some to take home
Ask at your hotel or the Rayong City tourism office for current producer tour arrangements

activities.name: Visit Rayong Botanical Garden
activities.description: A large public botanical garden near Rayong City featuring diverse plant collections, scenic hiking trails, a tranquil lake, and a waterfall. The garden spans hundreds of rai of former rubber plantation land and serves as the city's green lung. Weekend events and orchid exhibitions make it a pleasant half-day stop if you're in Rayong City.
tips: The garden is at its best early morning with good birdwatching opportunities
The hilltop viewpoint is a 30-minute walk from the entrance and offers views over the surrounding forest
Located 10 km west of Rayong City on Route 36 — needs private transport

activities.name: Explore Rayong City's Old Town
activities.description: Rayong City's old town center along Sukhumvit Road has a handful of well-preserved Chinese shophouses, the Rayong City Pillar Shrine (Lak Mueang), and excellent local restaurants serving the eastern Thai specialty khao man gai (poached chicken rice) and tom yum goong that rivals Bangkok. The city is completely off the foreign tourist track — a genuine window into provincial Thai urban life.
tips: The morning fresh market near the city clock tower opens at 5 AM — the best time to see local life
The Lak Mueang (city pillar shrine) on Sukhumvit Road is an important local place of worship — dress respectfully
Rayong City food is cheaper than Ban Phe by 20-30% — eat lunch here before driving to the coast

activities.name: Watch the Fire Shows at Hat Sai Kaew
activities.description: Every evening at Hat Sai Kaew (White Sand Beach) on Koh Samet, beach bars put on fire shows — juggling, poi spinning, and fire breathing by professional performers. The shows run roughly 9-11 PM and are a free spectacle for anyone on the beach. Combined with the Milky Way visible over the Gulf on clear nights far from city light pollution, the beach transforms into one of Thailand's most memorable evening scenes.
activities.price: Free (drinks at beach bars from 80-150 THB)
tips: The fire shows are best on Friday and Saturday evenings when more performers are active
Buy drinks from the beach bar closest to the show — they set up the stages in return for drinks revenue
Arrive by 8:30 PM for good viewing position — shows start around 9 PM

activities.name: Kayak Along Mae Ramphueng Beach
activities.description: Rent a kayak from vendors on Mae Ramphueng Beach and paddle along the 12 km coastline. The calm, shallow Gulf water makes this ideal for beginners and families. The flat beach horizon and low coastal development give it an almost wild, uninterrupted feel. Paddle north toward Ban Phe for the most scenic stretch.
tips: Morning is the calmest — afternoons can be slightly windy
The beach is completely flat — you can see for kilometers and easily judge distance
Check the kayak condition before taking it out — rentals vary in quality

activities.name: Try Eastern Thai Cooking
activities.description: Eastern Thai cuisine shares characteristics with central Thai but uses more fresh seafood, more pla ra (fermented fish), and stronger flavors. Try a cooking class in Ban Phe or Rayong City to learn to make regional specialties: hoy op wunsen (clams with glass noodles), pla rad prik (whole fish with chili sauce), and gaeng som (sour curry with vegetables and fresh fish). Classes use ingredients sourced from the Ban Phe morning market.
tips: Book through your hotel — most arrange classes on request
Ask to focus on seafood dishes using local Gulf catches rather than the standard tourist menu
A market tour before cooking adds authenticity and teaches you to identify local ingredients

activities.name: Sunset at Laem Mae Phim Headland
activities.description: The Laem Mae Phim promontory at the southern end of Mae Ramphueng Beach offers one of the Eastern Seaboard's best sunset viewpoints — a rocky headland with 180-degree Gulf of Thailand views and the silhouettes of Koh Samet and the outer islands visible on the horizon. A small temple sits on the point and a few seafood restaurants have tables with sunset views.
tips: The headland is at the very tip of Laem Mae Phim — follow the coastal road to the end
The small temple on the point is working — remove shoes and dress respectfully
Arrive 30 minutes before sunset to get a good spot — the viewpoint gets surprisingly busy on weekends

travelTips: Koh Samet is accessible year-round unlike the Andaman island destinations — ferries run even during monsoon season (May-Oct)
The national park fee (200 THB for foreigners) is collected at the pier before boarding your ferry — keep your receipt as it's valid for one week
Mae Ramphueng Beach is 12 km long — a scooter or bicycle rental makes sense if you want to explore more than one section
Tropical fruit season (May-July) is genuinely one of the best reasons to visit — prices at roadside stalls are 30-50% of Bangkok supermarket prices
Bring reef-safe sunscreen for Koh Samet — chemical sunscreens are banned in national park waters and enforced with fines
